Scenario:
` XML:
```xml
SolarWinds-Inspired Advanced Persistent Threat Against Healthcare Sector
Healthcare and Public Health Complex The threat actor, associated with the SolarWinds Compromise, aims to infiltrate and maintain persistence within the target healthcare organization's network, ultimately leading to data destruction.
name=SolarWinds Compromise Financial Gain / Espionage Expert
Healthcare and Public Health Internet-facing assets Web
AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Shared DB or directory, Vulnerability, Weak passwords, Improper configuration, Obsolete products, Injection flaws, Custom APIs, Authentication issues AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Web, Vulnerability exploit, Phishing, Brute-force attack, Social engineering, Application compromise
Open-Source Intelligence Internet-facing assets Web N/A CAPEC ID: 538 - Open-Source Library Manipulation The threat actor gathers public information about the target healthcare organization to identify potential entry points and valuable assets.
Active Scanning Internet-facing assets Web N/A CAPEC ID: 309 - Network Topology Mapping The threat actor scans the target organization's internet-facing assets to discover vulnerable systems and services.
Custom Malware Development Internet-facing assets Web N/A CAPEC ID: 673 - Developer Signing Maliciously Altered Software The threat actor develops tailored malware, similar to Sunburst/Solorigate, to be deployed during the initial access phase.
Living-off-the-Land Tool Development Internet-facing assets Web N/A CAPEC ID: 116 - Excavation The threat actor creates tools to blend in with normal system activity and evade detection during post-exploitation phases.
Supply Chain Compromise Internet-facing assets Web CVE-2010-4345 [CWE-502]: Apache Log4j2 JNDI features used in configuration... CAPEC ID: 439 - Manipulation During Distribution The threat actor gains initial access to the target network through a trusted third-party software provider, exploiting a vulnerability in the software's update mechanism.
The SolarWinds Compromise-associated threat actor employs a complex attack strategy involving reconnaissance, resource development, initial access via supply chain compromise, and multiple post-exploitation stages to maintain persistence and ultimately destroy data within the targeted healthcare organization's network.
